title: attach.go handleAttach setup: defer unlock
description:
File: internal/session/attach.go, lines 44-86

handleAttach() locks the VT mutex for the initial attach setup: setting up per-client output, OSC colors, resizing the PTY, re-rendering existing clients, enabling mouse reporting, and rendering the current screen. The unlock is manual at line 86.

This is called from handleConn() which has a recover() (listener.go:30). However, the recover catches the panic without releasing the VT mutex — so the goroutine survives but the mutex stays locked, deadlocking the entire session silently. This is arguably worse than crashing.

Current risk: Long critical section calling Resize(), ForEachClient(), RenderScreen(), RenderBar(). Any panic deadlocks the session.

Fix:
1. Convert manual vt.Mu.Unlock() to defer-based unlock (wrap in anonymous func).
2. No additional recover needed — handleConn already has one. With defer unlock, the mutex will be properly released before the recover catches the panic.
